purpose: Define authoritative naming rules for module symbols, domain prefixes, ordinals, capability names, file names, and contract names in the EAFIX system. Recovered from EAFIX_auth_docs/Mod_refactor_plan.txt (deleted in commit 0f2c238).
authority_note: This file governs naming conventions only. Actual module identities remain in module_catalog.json.

module_symbol_pattern.description: All canonical module symbols follow this structure.

domain_prefix_table.domain: EA-side MQL4 internal modules (Pass 2 only, pending MT4/Python boundary resolution)
domain_prefix_table.governance_rule: Do not create EA-prefix modules until the MT4/Python authority boundary is resolved.

domain_prefix_table.domain: Governance/documentation tooling (if later treated as modules)

ordinal_rules.description: The ordinal number is stable within a prefix domain. It does not encode process order.

rules.case: Existing module renamed to resolve a collision
rules.rule: Assign a new symbol with a new ordinal; old symbol becomes a legacy_alias

rules.case: New module split from existing module
rules.rule: Use the next open ordinal in the same prefix domain
Do not reuse the ordinal, even after retirement
Keep the surviving module's ordinal; the retired module's symbol becomes a legacy_alias/deprecated entry
Use CANDIDATE-<description> notation, not a real ordinal

capability_name_rules.description: The CAPABILITY_NAME part of the symbol must be a noun phrase describing what the module produces or owns, not what it does.
rules: The name should describe the module's output or owned concern.
Avoid helper, manager, utils, thing as capability names.
Multi-word names use underscores: CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER not CALENDARSOURCEADAPTER.

manifests.separator: double underscore between ID and symbol for easy parsing

contract_naming_rules.description: Contract names describe the data structure being passed, not the action being performed.
contract_naming_rules.versioning_rule: Append a version suffix only when the schema actually changes incompatibly: RiskDecision_v1, RiskDecision_v2, OrderIntent_v1_2. Do not version contracts that have not changed.

numeric_id_rules.description: Numeric module IDs are opaque permanent handles. They must not encode semantic meaning.
rules: Use the numeric ID (^5\d{19}$) as an opaque permanent handle only.
Use the canonical symbol for human-readable meaning.
Do not attempt to make numeric IDs encode layer, phase, group, runtime, or order.
If numeric IDs were made to encode meaning, every architecture refactor would create pressure to renumber, which breaks history.

example.bad: Making the ID encode that a module is in layer 3 or phase R

collision_avoidance_rules.description: Two modules must never share a prefix+ordinal combination.

known_collision.description: F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES (id 50000000000000000001) and F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR (id 50000000000000000025) both use F1. Mod_refactor_plan.txt documents that F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR is the intended rename target via a future RENAME_SYMBOL MCR.

collision_avoidance_rules.resolution_process: File a RENAME_SYMBOL MCR. Old symbol becomes legacy_alias. Numeric ID does not change.
